All 18 source imports were confirmed to use the new package with no legacy references remaining. Four stale documentation files still referencing deleted `lib/llm/providers/` paths and the removed `UnifiedInferenceEngine` class were updated: `docs/subscription-providers-implementation.md`, `docs/knowledge-management/README.md`, `docs/knowledge-management/continuous-learning-system.md`, and `docs/knowledge-management/system-comparison.md`. MkDocs nav, PUML diagrams, and PNGs were already current and required no changes.

theme: Transcript Monitor Death Spiral Diagnosis and Fix
summary: An extended multi-session investigation diagnosed why the observation/transcript monitor stopped recording after 6:42 and why the post-recording artifact patch was not functioning. The root cause was a cascading failure: `better-sqlite3` failed to load in the ESM context, falling back to a `sqlite3` CLI call that timed out on the 2GB OpenCode database, causing `hasNewContent()` to always return false, triggering idle timeout exits, and then coordinator restarts — creating a death spiral. Additional bugs discovered included an `ENOBUFS` pipe buffer overflow from ~27MB of sqlite3 subprocess output, a bare `execSync` reference that should have been `_execSync`, an `Object.entries` crash on an undefined PSM global registry, and 26+ duplicate monitor processes spawned by the coordinator. Fixes applied to `scripts/enhanced-transcript-monitor.js` replaced `better-sqlite3` with a corrected sqlite3 CLI fallback scoped to recent messages, patched the `_execSync` reference, and added a guard for the undefined PSM registry. Two bugs in `global-lsl-coordinator.js` and `process-state-manager.js` were also fixed: a duplicate `projectName` variable declaration syntax error and `readRegistry()` crashing on missing or empty registry files. After killing the 26 runaway processes and restarting cleanly, both the coding and rapid-automations monitors ran without duplicates and LSL files were confirmed writing to the correct `coding/.specstory/history/` location. All todo items were marked complete upon resolution.
filesTouched: process-state-manager.js (fixed readRegistry to handle missing/empty registry file with proper error handling and structure validation)

theme: LSL Acronym and File Location Clarification
summary: A clarification was made regarding the LSL (Live Session Logging) acronym and file storage conventions. LSL files are stored in `.specstory/history` (or `~/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history`) with filenames following a convention ending in `*_from-rapid-automation`. This corrected a prior misunderstanding about what LSL referred to.

theme: Root Cause Investigation of Stale ETM Registry Path
summary: Investigated degraded tmux health status and missing observations since the previous day. Traced the root cause to the LSL coordinator registry mapping `rapid-automations` to the shadow directory `~/Agentic/rapid-automations` instead of the real repo at `~/Agentic/_work/rapid-automations`, causing ETM to find no matching OpenCode sessions. The health system failed to catch this because it only checks process liveness, not observation recency. Three fixes were proposed: path validation with `realpathSync` and `.git` check in `ensure()`, an observation-recency health check, and immediate registry correction with ETM restart — none yet implemented at this point.

theme: ETM pollCount Hardening and Dead Code Removal
summary: Across several overlapping continuation sessions, the ETM health writer was updated to emit a `pollCount` metric enabling coordinator phantom detection. Both the rapid-automations and coding ETMs were restarted to pick up the change. In parallel, dead code was removed from the coding project's `SemanticAnalyzer.js` (`mergeTrajectoryWithSession` and `parseTrajectoryResponse` methods, which referenced unset `this.baseURL` and `this.apiKey`) and from `trajectory-analyzer.js` (vestigial `getApiKeyForProvider`, `getBaseUrlForProvider`, `initializeFastInference`, and unused `apiKey`/`baseUrl` fields, all made redundant by the localhost proxy inference path). The global LSL coordinator (`global-lsl-coordinator.js`) was also hardened with path revalidation. A classification log audit confirmed the routing is correct at roughly 75% LOCAL and 25% CODING decisions, all resolved at the embedding layer or earlier.
filesTouched: SemanticAnalyzer (removed mergeTrajectoryWithSession and parseTrajectoryResponse dead methods)
trajectory analyzer (removed dead provider URL/key logic)
rapid-automations ETM health writer to include pollCount metric
coding SemanticAnalyzer to remove mergeTrajectoryWithSession and parseTrajectoryResponse
coding trajectory analyzer to remove trajectory-analyzer/initializeFastInference dead methods and unused apiKey/baseUrl fields

theme: Read-Only Architecture Survey of Coding Project
summary: Conducted a read-only investigation across the `~/Agentic/coding` project's docs, scripts, and src directories to build a comprehensive understanding of the system architecture. No files were modified. The survey covered observation generation, the 5-layer LSL classification pipeline, foreign prompt set redirection, ETM behavior, reclassification scripts, and health check mechanisms.

theme: Shadow Folder Cleanup and LSL Reclassification Attempt
summary: Investigated three issues: shadow folder removal, missing observations, and misclassified CODING LSLs in rapid-automations. The shadow folder was confirmed already deleted. Observations were verified working correctly with 1,197 entries in the central DB including fresh rapid-automations entries. The LSL reclassification effort stalled — the batch processor's foreign-only mode has a hardcoded September 2025 date filter blocking historical reprocessing, and a missing submodule caused ENOENT errors. After a crash, context was recovered by reading session logs, confirming shadow folder and observations tasks complete, but the batch reclassification of historically misclassified LOCAL LSLs remained unexecuted.

theme: Frontend Dashboard Observations Display Debugging
summary: Debugged observations not appearing in the frontend dashboard, which appeared to show data stopping at Apr 20. Investigation traced the full stack: the `.observations` directory was initially not mounted in the Docker container (later confirmed to be correctly populated with current data inside the container), the internal API correctly returns current entries (62 for Apr 21), CORS is fine, and the built dist is current. Two candidate root causes were identified but not fixed: a frontend field name mismatch (`timestamp` vs `created_at`) and a potential chart rendering limitation from using only the first 50 results. No fix was implemented by end of session.

theme: Fix Observations Dashboard Auto-Refresh Date Window
summary:
The observations dashboard was failing to display current data when the page remained open past midnight, because the `to` date filter was computed once at page load and never updated.

- **Root cause:** Auto-refresh interval reused a stale `to` date, excluding any observations after the initial page load timestamp
- **Fix:** Modified `observations.tsx` to recompute `to` as the current date on every 30-second polling cycle
- Rebuilt the frontend dist and restarted the static server to deploy the change

The dashboard now correctly extends its query window to the current time on each refresh, ensuring today's observations always appear without a manual reload.

theme: Session Initialization and GSD Tool Update
summary: Session began by loading recent LSL files spanning March 29–April 22, 2026 to establish context continuity across the coding and rapid-automations projects. A GSD version check was then performed, detecting an upgrade from v1.34.2 to v1.38.3. After displaying the changelog (covering new skills, runtime support, and bug fixes across versions 1.35.0–1.38.3), the update was executed via clean install. `ObservationWriter.js` was touched as part of these startup steps.

theme: Duplicate Observations Investigation in ObservationWriter
summary: Investigated duplicate entries appearing on the dashboard. Root cause identified in `ObservationWriter.js`: the deduplication logic uses Jaccard similarity at a 0.7 threshold over a 10-observation sliding window, but LLM re-phrasing of identical work produces sufficient lexical variation to bypass the check. This resulted in approximately 46 duplicate entries spread across 3–4 cognitive clusters.

theme: ObservationConsolidator Design and Implementation
summary:
Researched Mastra's three-level memory compression hierarchy (messages → observations → reflections) to identify a gap in the coding ecosystem's single-level observation system. Proposed and then implemented a two-tier aggregation layer mirroring Mastra's Observer/Reflector pattern, but adapted to be time-triggered and cross-agent rather than token-threshold and per-thread:
- **Digests** — daily thematic consolidation of raw observations
- **Insights** — weekly persistent project knowledge synthesis
- New DB tables, an `ObservationConsolidator` class, and dashboard server API endpoints were implemented in `~/Agentic/coding/src/live-logging/ObservationConsolidator.js`, integrating with the existing live-logging system.

- `integrations/system-health-dashboard/src/pages/insights.tsx`

Discovered that local `main` and `origin/main` diverged approximately 3 months prior (January 19, 2026), with local main 1040 commits ahead and 741 behind — unrelated to current session work. Resolution was to push to a feature branch instead of main.

Subsequent attempts to trigger the "Deploy Documentation" GitHub Actions workflow after a force-push were unsuccessful automatically; `gh` CLI lacked `github.com` authentication, requiring manual trigger via the browser Actions tab.

Separately, MkDocs `--strict` mode surfaced fatal build errors due to broken internal links in `docs-content/architecture/llm-architecture.md` referencing the non-existent `llm-proxy-bridge.md`. Fixed by updating all references to point to the correct existing file `llm-cli-proxy.md`, then committed to re-trigger the deploy workflow.

theme: GitHub Submodule Access Issue and Copi Removal
summary:
Investigated a GitHub issue filed on February 22 reporting submodule access failures for external contributors. Root cause: submodule URLs use SSH (`<EMAIL_REDACTED>:`) and the `copi` repo is private, causing "Repository not found" errors; `mcp-constraint-monitor` and `memory-visualizer` appear to now be public.

Investigation into `copi` revealed it is a legacy GitHub Copilot CLI wrapper with session logging and tmux integration — no longer imported or used at runtime, as its functionality was extracted into `scripts/capture-monitor.js` and the ETM.

The `integrations/copi` submodule was removed via `git rm`, which also cleared its `.gitmodules` entry. Cleanup of `.git/config` and `.git/modules/integrations/copi` was initiated but the exchange ended before a final commit was made.

theme: Auto-Consolidation Daemon Design and Wiring Decision
summary:
Investigated whether a daemon should automatically trigger observation consolidation when a threshold of new observations accumulates. Key findings across the review:
- Daemon logic exists inside `consolidate-observations.js` but is never started
- No trigger hook exists where observations are written (`ObservationWriter`)
- `docker-compose.yml` was referenced as an artifact in all three related observations

Outcome: proposed adding auto-consolidation directly to the existing health API server — a periodic check every 30 minutes that triggers consolidation when undigested observation count for completed days exceeds a configured threshold. No implementation was committed.

theme: Constraint Dashboard and FATAL Service Crash Diagnosis and Fix
summary:
Three FATAL supervisord processes (`constraint-monitor`, `semantic-analysis`, `constraint-dashboard`) were diagnosed and the dashboard's port 3030 serving problem was fully resolved.
- Confirmed `constraint-dashboard` was mapped on port 3030 in docker-compose but nothing was listening inside the container — `dashboard-server.js` only serves the API on port 3031
- Root cause: `supervisord` only started `dashboard-server.js`, not the Next.js frontend app located at `integrations/mcp-constraint-monitor/dashboard/`
- **Fix (obs 32):**
  - `docker/Dockerfile.coding-services`: added `npm install` and Next.js build steps for the dashboard
  - `docker/supervisord.conf`: split into two processes — `constraint-dashboard-api` (port 3031) and a new Next.js frontend process (port 3030)
  - `config/health-verification-rules.json` and `config/health-verifier.js`: updated to check both ports independently
- No stderr logs were found for the crashing services; stdout logs were the next investigation target

theme: Knowledge Graph IssueTriage UI Bug Fixes
summary:
Two distinct bugs in the knowledge graph viewer's `IssueTriage` component were investigated and one was fixed.
- **Entity key format mismatch (obs 28):** edges used `layer:id` prefixed keys while `IssueTriage` looked up bare `id` keys, causing silent lookup failures; identified but not yet fixed
- **Missing RCA chain (obs 34, 35):** `Cluster10_ArgoWorkflowStopped` showed 0 RCA chain entries because it was a genuinely isolated node with 0 actual edges despite appearing to have 45 connections
- **Evidence bloat (obs 33, 34, 35):** BFS hop distance set too large (3 hops across 900+ nodes) combined with domain-based fuzzy matching pulling all entities in the catch-all `"general"` domain inflated evidence sources to 148+
- **Fix applied:** `IssueTriage.tsx` updated to skip domain-based evidence collection when the entity's domain is `"general"`, preventing hundreds of unrelated evidence links
- Primary artifact: `~/Agentic/_work/rapid-automations/integrations/operational-knowledge-management/src/components/KnowledgeGraph/IssueTriage.tsx`

theme: Supervisord Config Reverted to Known-Good State

- Updated `config/health-verification-rules.json` to check port 3031 only, removing the erroneous `dashboard_api` entry
- Key architectural distinction re-established: Docker handles the API backend; host handles the interactive Next.js dev UI

theme: Docker Port Misconfiguration and Unauthorized Port Cleanup
summary:
A Docker Compose restart introduced port mapping errors, leading to a cascade of incorrect port choices.

- Port 3002 was incorrectly remapped to 3022 during restart; initial response was to serve on 3022 and persist `VOKB_PORT=3022` in `~/Agentic/_work/rapid-automations/.env`
- Developer intervened, requiring all ports to be sourced exclusively from the shared `.env.ports` file rather than hardcoded or invented
- The unauthorized `VOKB_PORT=3022` entry was removed from `.env`, restoring central port governance
- Multiple `package.json` files and `supervisord.conf` were touched during the churn; lesson recorded: no port values outside `.env.ports`

theme: IssueTriage Domain Sweep Over-Scoping Fixed
summary:
Investigated and fixed the `IssueTriage` component pulling excessively broad evidence for isolated knowledge graph nodes.

- A `raas` domain incident with 0 edges was collecting all `raas`-domain entities with ops refs — far beyond the prior `general` domain patch
- Root cause: domain sweep logic was too permissive for isolated nodes; BFS already handles connected evidence and `sourceRefs` cover isolated nodes directly
- Fix: removed domain sweep logic entirely from `~/Agentic/_work/rapid-automations/integrations/operational-knowledge-management/src/components/KnowledgeGraph/IssueTriage.tsx`
- Debug `console.log` spam also cleaned up in the same pass

theme: Constraint Dashboard Docker vs Host Placement Decision
summary:
Addressed architectural question of whether the constraint dashboard should run in Docker or on the host.

- Analyzed the existing Docker/host split to surface the implicit criterion: Docker for headless backend services and APIs; host only for tools requiring genuine host resources (CLIs, host networking) or hot-reload dev UIs
- Recommendation: move constraint dashboard into Docker so it works automatically after `docker-compose up`
- `constraint-dashboard.tsx` was touched as part of the investigation

theme: Ops Refs Propagation for Documentation-Sourced RCA Entities
summary:
Investigated why entities like "Job Failed — RPU Execution Error" show no operational references (Argo/RaaS/Grafana links) in the graph viewer.

- Root cause: these entities were ingested from mkdocs documentation rather than RCA findings, so no operational evidence was ever linked at ingest time
- 109 RCA-relevant entities share this gap; only 1 has a direct neighbor with ops refs, making single-hop propagation insufficient
- Proposed fix: BFS-based data-level propagation of ops refs through graph neighbors (2-hop) rather than patching display-time logic
- Investigation was read-only across these observations; `constraint-dashboard.tsx` was the primary artifact examined

theme: Port 3030 Dashboard Not Serving — Missing Next.js Process
summary:
Debugged a connection error on port 3030 where the dashboard UI was unreachable. Investigation of docker-compose port mappings and dashboard source revealed that while port 3030 is correctly mapped, no process inside the container listens on it. The Next.js app at `integrations/mcp-constraint-monitor/dashboard/` should serve on 3030, but `supervisord` only starts `dashboard-server.js` (the API on 3031) — the Next.js app is never launched.
- Edited `temporal.ts` and `routes.ts` during investigation.

theme: Knowledge Graph RCA Chain and Evidence Source UI Bugs
summary:
Three consecutive sessions investigating visual bugs in the knowledge graph issue triage view. Two root causes were identified and one resolved:
- **RCA chain missing**: `Cluster10_ArgoWorkflowStopped` appeared to have 45 connections but was actually an isolated node with 0 real edges, so no RCA chain could be surfaced.
- **Inflated evidence sources**: The BFS evidence collector uses domain-based fuzzy matching with a 3-hop radius across 900+ nodes; entities falling into the "general" catch-all domain pull 148+ unrelated evidence sources.
- **Stale JS bundle / browser cache**: A UI fix was present in the Docker-served bundle but was masked by browser caching; resolved by recommending a hard refresh (Cmd+Shift+R).
- Files edited: `IssueTriage.tsx`, `system-health-dashboard.tsx`, `health-verifier.js`, `health-verification-rules.json`, `supervisord.conf`, `Dockerfile.coding-services`.

theme: Diagnosing and Resolving Pre-commit Hook Blocking Commits
summary:
The developer was blocked from committing due to a pre-commit hook called "OKB BASELINE GUARD" enforcing that `.data/exports/knowledge-export/` (or `.data/knowledge-export/`) KB export files must not be staged alongside other changes.

- Root cause: pre-commit hook in the `operational-knowledge-management` submodule rejecting mixed staged changes
- Initial fix provided: unstage KB files and commit them independently via two command sequences
- Longer-term strategy decided: revise the hook logic to **allow** commits where only KB files are staged, and **block** only when KB files are mixed with non-KB changes — eliminating any commit message convention requirement
- No code changes were applied to the hook itself during these observations

theme: Phase 28 Embedding Pipeline Implementation and Debugging

**Files created:**

- `src/embedding-service.ts` — core embedding service
- `src/content-hash.ts` — SHA-256 idempotency hashing
- `src/qdrant-collections.ts` — Qdrant collection definitions
- Backfill CLI script — for existing data ingestion
- Redis pub/sub listener — write-time hook for new items
- `supervisord` and `docker-compose` config files

**Results:**
- 1,464 items backfilled across 4 Qdrant collections: `observations=645`, `digests=132`, `insights=12`, `kg_entities=675`
- Write-time hooks auto-embed new observations via Redis pub/sub events

**Post-execution debugging** identified two Docker startup failures: fastembed's platform-specific tokenizer binaries were blocked by `--ignore-scripts` during install, and `embedding-config.json` path resolution broke at runtime because `__dirname` pointed to `dist/embedding/` rather than `src/embedding/`. Fixes were in progress but not finalized.

A manual end-to-end verification procedure was also provided: check Qdrant `observations` point count, trigger a new Claude session to generate an observation, then recheck the count to confirm the write-time hook fires correctly.

theme: Debugging Knowledge Injection Hook Silent Failures

- `process.stdin.isTTY === false` strict equality never matched because piped stdin yields `undefined`, not `false` — changed to a truthy check
- `MIN_TOKENS` threshold of 20 was too high for real questions to pass — replaced with `MIN_WORDS = 4` to filter only trivial inputs like "yes" or "ok"

theme: Fixing Observation Timestamps, Classification, and Duplicate LSL Entries

**Fixes applied** (observation 2) across `ObservationWriter.js` and `enhanced-transcript-monitor.js`:
- Backfilled observations were using `Date.now()` instead of original message timestamps — corrected to preserve historical accuracy
- Project classification was reading only the first exchange (`[0]` hardcoded) instead of the full prompt set, defeating majority-vote logic — all 11 `determineTargetProject` call sites updated
- Stale flush paths were not updating `lastProcessedUuid`, causing duplicate LSL prompt set writes on re-processing — update now applied in all flush paths

**Verification attempt** (observation 3) to clean up incorrect LSL files, confirm dashboard visibility, and check Qdrant digest/insight sync ended mid-investigation with no corrective actions completed. Dashboard was tentatively located at port 3031 (Docker); LSL state, observations, digests, and Qdrant sync all remain unconfirmed.

theme: FTS5 Index Desync Breaks Observation Search Results
summary:
Both observations address the same issue: a screenshot showing "No matching observations" in the search UI prompted investigation into why backfilled observations weren't appearing in search results.

- Root cause: `ObservationWriter.js` never populates the FTS5 table, and no triggers exist to auto-sync it with the main `observations` table
- Symptom: FTS5 index row count was out of sync (675 vs 674 rows), causing backfilled observations to be invisible to search
- Immediate fix: manually rebuilt the FTS5 index in `observations.db` to restore search results
- Follow-up identified: `ObservationWriter.init()` needs FTS sync logic added to prevent future desyncs on backfill or any write path that bypasses the index

theme: Fix SQLite Corruption in observations.db Permanently

- Root cause identified: 6 different consumers opening the same DB with inconsistent settings — missing WAL mode, no `busy_timeout`, no integrity checks
- Solution: a single shared `SafeDatabase.js` module that enforces consistent configuration for all consumers
- `SafeDatabase.js` now enforces WAL mode, `busy_timeout`, integrity checks, and SHM recovery on every connection to `observations.db`
- Artifact: `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/src/live-logging/SafeDatabase.js`

theme: ObservationConsolidator Path Redaction Bug Investigation
summary:
Three successive observations investigated a redaction bug in `ObservationConsolidator.js` where file paths were being corrupted during processing.

- Root cause: the full `_redact()` function was being applied to structured metadata (file paths), causing false positives — specifically, a broad AWS Secret regex matching 40-character alphanumeric strings was corrupting valid path components
- Fix identified: only targeted user ID normalization should be applied to file paths, consistent with how the LSL system handles redaction elsewhere; full redaction should be restricted to free-text fields
- `ObservationConsolidator.js` was edited across all three observations as the investigation progressed

theme: Diagnosing Irrelevant Knowledge Context Injection
summary:

- Investigation traced the retrieval pipeline from `knowledge-injection-hook` through `retrieval-client` to the retrieval service on port 3033
- Root cause narrowed to Qdrant likely being non-functional, causing fallback to keyword search that produced poor matches
- Embedding similarity scores were found to cluster too tightly (0.75–0.82 range), making topic discrimination unreliable

theme: Improving Retrieval Relevance with Context Enrichment and Topic Filtering

- Identified that MiniLM embedding scores were too compressed to distinguish topics, and that existing Qdrant payload filtering, Memgraph graph traversal, and ontology infrastructure were all unused
- Recommended and approved combining **conversation-context query enrichment** with **Qdrant topic pre-filtering** as the highest-impact, lowest-effort fix
- Updated `knowledge-injection-hook.js` to extract topics from the conversation transcript and pass them to the retrieval service for Qdrant-filtered searches
- At the time of observation 5, noise had already dropped somewhat: 4 insights and 10 digests injected, but ~80% still irrelevant; per-tier caps awaited a Docker rebuild of `token-budget.js`

theme: Fixing Server Crash Due to Top-Level Await

theme: Auditing and Fixing Context Injection Dedup and Working Memory
summary:
A series of context audits revealed persistent noise issues even after earlier retrieval tuning, leading to several targeted fixes across the injection pipeline.

- Noise reduced from ~80% to ~40% irrelevant after retrieval tuning, but three recurring issues persisted: duplicate "OKB Architecture" insights at differing confidence scores (broken dedup), a malformed KG entity name `PromiseHandlesAsynchronousOperations`, and recursive `<COMPANY_NAME_REDACTED>`-question observations being re-indexed and re-injected
- **Transcript feedback loop fix** (commit `800d0fdc`): `knowledge-injection-hook.js` updated to skip `system-reminder` blocks when extracting transcript topics, breaking the cycle where injected content was re-read as user context
- **Working memory fix**: `working-memory.js` updated to prefer the canonical "Coding" project entity rather than defaulting to the first result in a bloated 673-entry KG query of misclassified "Project" entities
- **Dedup fix**: near-duplicate insight deduplication added to `token-budget.js` to collapse semantically identical insights; duplicate OKB Architecture insights reduced from 2 to 1
- All three fixes committed as `fdb41381`

theme: Knowledge Graph Audit, Cleanup Planning, and API Filter Fixes
summary:
A structured plan was developed and partially executed to make the knowledge graph reliable, uncovering and fixing multiple bugs in the entity retrieval API along the way.

- **Cleanup plan** (6 phases): Audit → Fix producers → Cleanup script → Qdrant resync → Guards → Acceptance test; existing `purge-knowledge-entities.js` was surveyed as a starting point
- **Audit finding**: the VKB API `?type=` filter was entirely non-functional; KG actually contained 673 correctly-typed entities (1 Project, 8 Component, 353 SubComponent, 310 Detail, 1 System) — not misclassified as assumed
- **API parameter mismatch bug**: `/api/entities` route expected `types` (plural, CSV) but `working-memory.js` called it with `type` (singular); fixed in `api-routes.js` to accept both for backward compatibility
- **GraphDatabaseService bug**: Project nodes were omitted from `?type=Project` <USER_ID_REDACTED> due to second-pass deduplication logic; fixed in `GraphDatabaseService.js`
- Docker container for `coding-services` rebuilt and restarted to deploy the `GraphDatabaseService` fix (not bind-mounted)
- **Client-side leak fix**: `working-memory.js` updated to filter out Project/System nodes leaking into component-type result lists
- End state: working memory correctly shows the Coding project with 8 real Components, no duplicates, no System entity leakage

theme: Deduplicating Insights via Embedding Similarity Thresholds
summary:
Goal was to fix near-duplicate insights slipping through the consolidation pipeline because `ObservationConsolidator.js` used exact SQL topic matching rather than vector similarity, allowing LLM-reworded duplicates to persist.

- Root cause identified: no Qdrant embedding lookup before insight insertion
- Proposed two-part fix: one-time cleanup script + consolidator patch to query Qdrant pre-insert
- `dedup-insights-by-embedding.js` iteratively refined — moved from single-linkage chaining to greedy pairwise merging after chaining caused unrelated insights to merge transitively
- Similarity threshold raised to **0.93+** after empirical analysis showed MiniLM cosine floor is ~0.89–0.92, making 0.90 too aggressive
- Final result: insights collection reduced from 19 → 16 by merging three clean duplicate clusters at ≥0.93 cosine similarity

theme: Deduplicating Digests via Embedding Similarity
summary: Extended the embedding-based dedup approach to the digests collection via `dedup-digests-by-embedding.js`. Empirically tested thresholds against the digests collection and settled on **0.97** as the right cutoff — high enough to capture genuine LLM-reworded duplicates without false positives from shared project vocabulary. Script identified 47 duplicate clusters and reduced the collection from 298 → 251 digests.

theme: Fixing Overly Broad AWS Secret Redaction Pattern
summary:
Investigated a bug where file path segments were incorrectly matched by the AWS secret redaction pattern in `ConfigurableRedactor.js`.

- Root cause: `[a-zA-Z0-9+/]{40}` pattern is too broad, consuming any 40-character alphanumeric string including path segments
- The string `consuming` is itself exactly 40 chars and gets re-consumed by the pattern
- Investigation used LSL system's redaction logic as reference for applying boundary/context constraints
- Affected files: `redaction-patterns.json`, `ConfigurableRedactor.js`
- Fix was not yet applied at the point these observations were captured; root cause analysis was complete.

theme: Debugging and Fixing Broken Observation Dashboard Updates
summary:
Investigated why the observation dashboard stopped updating after 11:32 AM, uncovering two stacked bugs:

- **Deduplication hash collision**: `ObservationWriter.js` hashes empty `userContent`, causing every exchange in a session to collide to the same hash (`6d37c4ec`), silently discarding all generated summaries without writing to the DB.
- **Stale file handle**: The transcript-monitor process held a stale handle to a rotated/corrupted DB file, causing writes to land in the wrong file.

Fixed by adding inode-rotation detection to `ObservationWriter.js` so it automatically compares inodes and reopens the database connection when the underlying file changes, preventing the split-brain scenario between writer and reader.

theme: Embedding Listener Service Failure Diagnosis and Fix
summary:
A red status indicator on the system health dashboard triggered investigation into a failing service. Out of 12 health checks, only the `embedding-listener` supervisord process was failing with a FATAL state ("Exited too <USER_ID_REDACTED>"); all other services (Qdrant, VKB, constraint-monitor, etc.) were healthy.

- Investigated supervisor process status, error logs, and config files to identify root cause
- Attempted manual process execution to reproduce the failure
- Root cause identified: fastembed local cache was not available at runtime because the `local_cache` directory is not bind-mounted into the container
- Fix applied: moved fastembed cache pre-warming into the Docker builder stage so the cache is baked into the image and copied to production
- Files changed: `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/docker/supervisord.conf`, `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/docker/Dockerfile.coding-services`

theme: Fix fastembed cache and proxy config in Docker
summary:
Goal was to resolve `embedding-listener` startup failures (`FATAL`) in the Docker-based coding services environment.

- Pre-warmed the fastembed model cache during the Docker **builder stage** and copied it into the production image (~90MB baked in), since `local_cache` is not bind-mounted at runtime
- Fixed `supervisord` to **inherit proxy environment variables** rather than clearing them, with `NO_PROXY` configured to cover internal services
- Files changed: `docker/supervisord.conf`, `docker/Dockerfile.coding-services`

Result: `embedding-listener` now starts cleanly (`RUNNING`, no `FATAL`), health check reports healthy with `violationCount 0`, and external fetches work as a fallback via inherited proxy env.

theme: Progress Bar and ObservationConsolidator Runtime Bug Investigation
summary:
Two UI bugs were investigated from shared screenshots: a progress bar prematurely reaching 100% during active processing, and an intermittent runtime error in `ObservationConsolidator.js` around line 940–943.

- **Progress bar bug**: Root cause confirmed as a chunk ratio calculation error in `consolidation-progress.tsx` — the N/M ratio marks 100% at the *start* of a chunk rather than the *end*, causing the display to jump to completion prematurely.
- **Runtime error**: Observations diverged slightly between runs — one pass suspected a `close()`/singleton regression as the source of the intermittent error; a second diagnostic pass identified a concrete `SqliteError: database disk image is malformed` at line 940, pointing to actual corruption in `observations.db`.
- `consolidation-progress.tsx` was edited in both sessions as part of the investigation.
- The SQLite corruption finding supersedes the singleton theory and warrants direct inspection and repair of `observations.db`.

theme: SSH Authentication Failure with GitHub Enterprise
summary:

theme: Coding Services Container Idle CPU Diagnosis and Fix
summary:
Investigated persistent high CPU usage (~8-9%, later observed at 13-30%) in the `coding-services` Docker container during idle periods with no active clients.

**Diagnosis path:**
- Checked health files, supervisor/coordinator logs, and SSE stream behavior to narrow the source
- Used `docker stats` sampling and `top -bn2` delta-based per-PID profiling to identify internal timers
- Found a 100ms `setInterval` at `server.js:1031` firing 10×/second regardless of client connections
- PID 99 (`system-health-dashboard/server.js`) identified spiking to 6-8% on a ~12-14s cadence; PID 132 caught at 84% in a single tick

**Fixes applied across three commits:**
- `scripts/global-process-supervisor.js`: DEBUG file-write gate to reduce logging (~9MB/day saved)
- `scripts/health-prompt-hook.js`: cwd-robust LSL check to eliminate false alarms
- `integrations/system-health-dashboard/src/store/middleware/healthRefreshMiddleware.ts`: adaptive polling with visibility gate to cut idle CPU ~10×
- `integrations/system-health-dashboard/server.js`: SSE handler rewritten to stat-gate the 618KB JSON read on `mtimeMs+size`, adaptive intervals (100ms active / 1s idle), dead `watcher`/`debounceTimer` declarations removed — committed as `e1f12d7ab`, dropping idle CPU from ~5% to ~1.93%

Post-fix analysis confirmed baseline reduction; `code-graph-rag` Python SSE server identified as next optimization target. Intermittent 7-8% spikes on ~12-14s cadence remained under investigation at session end.

theme: Diagnosing Missing Repos and GHE SSH Auth Failure
summary:
After the workspace update, investigation continued into why only 4 of 6 repos were visible — `rapid-automations` and `rapid-automations-history` remained missing.

- Initial suspicion was a GitHub Enterprise (GHE) authentication problem
- VS Code's Git extension triggered auto-fetch using `<EMAIL_REDACTED>` as the SSH username rather than the standard `git@` user, causing a `publickey permission denied` error
- Further analysis found that remote URLs explicitly specifying `<EMAIL_REDACTED>` override the `User git` directive in the SSH config, breaking GHE auth for SSH remotes (HTTPS remotes unaffected)
- Direct SSH connection tests ruled out a simple user-override as the sole cause — `git@<COMPANY_NAME_REDACTED>.ghe.com` also failed, pointing to an underlying SSH key authentication issue with the GHE server itself
- No workspace artifacts were modified during this phase; investigation was ongoing

theme: Docker Container Downtime Root Cause Investigation
summary:
Investigated why the `coding-services` Docker container went down overnight and failed to auto-restart.

- **Root cause:** Docker Desktop was manually quit, cleanly shutting down the VM and container (exit code 0); the `unless-stopped` restart policy only triggers on daemon restart, requiring Docker Desktop to be relaunched manually.
- **Ruling out:** Mac reboot and container crash were both eliminated as causes via Docker logs, Mac uptime, and sleep/wake event analysis.
- **Secondary issue found:** `health-verifier` inside the container is in an ongoing crash-respawn loop (unrelated to the downtime).
- **Fix:** Enable "Start Docker Desktop when you sign in" in Docker Desktop settings to prevent recurrence.
- No files were modified during this investigation.

theme: Docker Container Recreating in Loop: Bind Mount Fix
summary:
A Docker container was being force-recreated every ~5 minutes due to a stale health verification rule referencing `scripts/consolidate-observations.js` in the `bind_mount_freshness.files` list — a file no longer bind-mounted after the obs-api cutover but still baked into the image at a mismatched size.

- **Root cause:** `health-verification-rules.json` was never updated after the bind-mount was removed, causing a permanent mismatch loop
- **Fix:** Removed the stale file reference from `bind_mount_freshness.files` in `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/config/health-verification-rules.json`
- **Verification:** Container `136c389e4f45` remained stable at 22 min uptime, zero heal attempts post-restart, 18/18 health checks passing with 0 violations; Task #4 marked complete

- LSL processes were confirmed running (PIDs verified, health files current)

theme: Root Cause Analysis of Health Monitoring Architecture Issues
summary:
Broad diagnostic pass across the health monitoring stack to explain why LSL showed red, why the observation system appeared broken, and why the dashboard falsely reported "all green."

- LSL red was a false alarm caused by stale `.health` JSON files
- Observation system was actually healthy; dashboard "all green" was honest but used incomplete rules missing container recreate counts and supervisord crash loops
- The `launchd → watchdog → supervisor → statusline` chain was flagged as over-engineered, actively obscuring real bugs by treating everything as a fallback
- Two remediation paths proposed: **tactical** (1-2hr symptom fixes) or **strategic** (single source of truth, narrowed auto-heal, dropped redundant layers)
- Modified `enhanced-transcript-monitor.js`

theme: Dashboard Unhealthy Due to Container Host Bind-Mount Mismatch
summary:
Investigated a discrepancy (evidenced by screenshots) where the dashboard reported unhealthy status despite the host reporting healthy.

- Root cause: dashboard reads `/coding/.health/verification-status.json` **inside the container**, which is not bind-mounted to the host's `.health/` directory
- The host writes to a separate `.health/` path, so the container sees stale or missing data and surfaces it as unhealthy
- Traced data flow through `server.js` and `verification-status.json` write paths to confirm the mismatch
- All three observations converge on the same diagnosis; `enhanced-transcript-monitor.js` was edited during investigation

theme: Port Liveness Panel Fixed via Category Property

theme: API Schema Mismatch Causing "No Check Data Yet" UI
summary:
Investigated why the dashboard UI displayed "no check data yet" despite the API correctly returning 23 checks.

- Port 3033 was returning 0 checks while port 3032 returned checks correctly; a JSON parsing issue around `data.checks` nesting added initial confusion
- Root cause traced to a missing `summary` field in the `/report` API response wrapper — the frontend store silently discarded the entire payload when this field was absent
- Fix targeted `server.js` to align the response schema with what the frontend parser expected

theme: Frontend Render Crash on Undefined `.length` Access Fixed
summary:
A runtime render error crashed the dashboard frontend after data began flowing through the system.

- Error was caused by the frontend accessing `.recommendations.length` on the API response object, which did not include that field
- Traced through built frontend assets to confirm the undefined access site
- Fix applied in `server.js`: added an empty `recommendations: []` array to the server response to satisfy the frontend's contract and prevent the crash

theme: Fix Invalid Time Value Errors in Dashboard Tables

The root cause was that `violations-table` and `system-checks-table` frontend components attempted to parse `.timestamp` fields on each row, but the server was not emitting those fields — resulting in undefined values being passed to date parsing logic with no null guards.

- Fix applied to `server.js` in the `system-health-dashboard` integration
- Added `.timestamp` fields to each violation and system-check row emitted by the server
- Resolved the `RangeError` by ensuring the frontend receives valid timestamp values rather than `undefined`

theme: TMux Status Line Display Bug Investigation and Fix
summary:
A long multi-hour debugging session focused on visual artifacts and alignment issues in the tmux right-side status bar, driven by iterative screenshot review and empirical testing.

**Root causes investigated and identified:**
- Cache poisoning from `status-line-fast.cjs`'s `.trim()` stripping padding and writing back unpadded content
- Cell-vs-codepoint width mismatch causing ghost characters
- tmux truncating `status-right` output at `min(status-right-length, pane_width - status_left)` and then stripping trailing ASCII spaces
- Trailing NBSP acting as content rather than padding, pushing the right edge left
- A structural ~3-cell gap at the right edge of `status-right` that cannot be written to

**Key changes made across the session to `combined-status-line.js` and supporting files:**
- Introduced `#[align=right]` directive (later reverted)
- Added brute-force trailing padding (later reverted)
- Passed `TMUX_PANE_WIDTH=#{pane_width}` via `.tmux.conf` so the script could calculate cell-aware padding
- Included pane width in cache key suffix (`-wNNN`) in `combined-status-line-wrapper.js`
- Moved padding from the end to the beginning of the string so tmux left-truncation removes padding not content
- Iteratively tuned the reserved-width constant (41 → 30 → 0)
- **Final resolution:** Removed all trailing NBSP/padding terminators from `combined-status-line.js`, accepting a small 1-2 cell structural gap and relying on tmux's native right-alignment

theme: ETM Duplicate Block Fixes and Per-Exchange Tranche Routing
summary:
Addressed two related LSL/ETM correctness problems in `enhanced-transcript-monitor.js`: duplicate prompt-set blocks accumulating in output files, and all prompt-set writes being anchored to the first exchange's time window.

- **Idempotency fix (obs 6):** Made `processUserPromptSetCompletion` idempotent by stripping prior `ps_${promptSetId}` blocks from all tranche part-files before appending the updated block, preventing files from growing past 200 KB and spawning unintended part files
- **File-lock fix (obs 9, 10):** Wrapped the remove-existing-block + append logic with `proper-lockfile` (already a PSM dependency) to prevent race conditions when multiple ETM instances run concurrently during the heartbeat warm-up period; stress-tested with 3 concurrent ETMs for 90+ seconds with no new duplicates
- **Per-exchange tranche routing (obs 7, 11, 14):** Redesigned so each exchange lands in the hourly file matching its own timestamp rather than the prompt set's start time, with `ps_id` as the linking key; long-running prompt sets now distribute across multiple hourly files, recoverable via grep
- `combined-status-line.js` read repeatedly for context/diagnosis during this session
- Two commits finalized: `865665502` (status-line width/cache/timer fixes in `scripts/status-line-fast.cjs`) and `22d456b0d` (per-exchange tranche routing in `scripts/enhanced-transcript-monitor.js`)

theme: Status Line Emoji/Unicode Width Rendering Bug Investigation and Fix
summary:
Extended investigation into a tmux status line rendering bug showing residue characters (e.g., `⚠️SYS:TIMEOUT6`, trailing `25` after timestamp) and misaligned cell widths.

- Initial diagnosis identified two issues: variable-width `⚠️` emoji causing trailing residue in the `[🔒 75%⚠️11]` fragment, and `combined-status-line.js` timing out 6+ consecutive times triggering emergency fallback display
- Developer selected option 2: strip emojis to single ASCII chars before cell-width counting in `leftPadToStableCellWidth`
- Implementation in `combined-status-line.js` proceeded but was partially reverted after developer objected to emojis being stripped from output (misinterpretation of the requirement)
- Root cause refined empirically: "Ambiguous" East Asian Width codepoints (`⚠`, `⚙`, `⏸`, `━`) were being counted as 2 cells by the script but tmux's `wcwidth` treats them as 1, causing a 1-cell miscalculation; `visibleCellWidth()` was corrected accordingly
- After further screenshots, a second layer was identified: tmux/xterm.js wcwidth mismatch where tmux counts emoji as 1 cell but VS Code's xterm.js renders them as 2 cells (tmux issues #836, #647) — diagnosed as a tmux config directive fix, not a script fix
- Primary artifact: `combined-status-line.js`; secondary: `status-line-fast.cjs`, `feedback_statusline_no_emoji_strip.md`, `MEMORY.md`

theme: Fixing Emoji Width Misalignment in tmux Terminal
summary:
Investigated persistent character residue accumulating in the terminal, but only within VS Code. Root cause identified as a wcwidth mismatch between tmux and xterm.js:
- tmux counts emoji as 1 cell wide by default
- xterm.js (used by VS Code) renders them as 2 cells wide
- This causes cursor drift and residue buildup over time

Modified `combined-status-line.js` and `status-line-fast.cjs` during investigation. Fix is to enable `variation-selector-always-wide` in tmux config (available since tmux 3.6), which aligns tmux's emoji width accounting with xterm.js rendering.

theme: Diagnosing Tmux Emoji Width Mismatch in VS Code Terminal
summary:
All three observations represent the same claude agent investigation into visual rendering artifacts (residue/drift) in the tmux status line when running inside VS Code's xterm.js terminal renderer.

- **Root cause**: cell-width disagreement between tmux's internal Unicode width tables and xterm.js for certain emoji characters
- Initial hypothesis pointed to `variation-selector-always-wide` being OFF by default, causing 1-cell drift for emoji+VS16 sequences
- Further investigation revealed the option was already enabled, shifting suspicion to **Unicode 13.0+ emoji** (e.g. 🪨 `U+1FAA8`) whose width tmux undercounts relative to xterm.js
- A prior scripted workaround was **reverted** after it introduced a new rendering disagreement
- No artifacts were modified; investigation was purely diagnostic and remains **ongoing**

summary:

- Fix applied to `combined-status-line.js`: added a residue buffer to the left-padding/cell-width calculation logic to absorb the drift
- Observation 27 followed up by verifying a `+2` `CELL_DRIFT_BUFFER` (or equivalent offset) was present in the rendering code; results updated in `STATE.md`, `MEMORY.md`, and phase-34 summary documents

theme: Insights System Refactor: Knowledge Docs Instead of Activity Logs
summary:
Large collaborative effort by opencode agents to diagnose and fix the insight generation pipeline, which was producing chronological activity logs rather than synthesized domain knowledge documents.

**Root causes identified:**
- ~587 entities hitting a fallback template path (LLM synthesis failing) that dumped raw bullet-point observations
- Bad entity names like `BugFixPattern`, `SemanticAnalysisWorkflowIssue` generated before naming guards existed
- No explicit anti-log instruction in LLM synthesis prompts

**Fixes applied** (primarily in `src/observation-generation-agent.ts`, `server/src/intelligence/knowledge-synthesis.ts`, and `src/insight-generator.ts`):
- Added `isProcessActivityName` filter to reject process/activity entity names at creation time
- Added negative naming examples to entity generation prompts
- Enhanced insight generation prompt to explicitly produce reference-style knowledge documents, not chronological logs
- Changed fallback template path to return `null` instead of writing low-<USER_ID_REDACTED> bullet-list files

**Build/deploy** (obs 21–28): Iteratively fixed TypeScript type errors introduced by the null-return changes — updated `generateInsightContent`, `generateInsightDocument`, and `generateTechnicalDocumentation` return types to `string | null` / `InsightDocument | null`, handled null at all call sites, corrected log level `"warn"` → `"warning"`. Build succeeded and service deployed healthy via Docker.

Existing bad-named entities still require a purge-and-batch-refresh to fully clean up the insights dashboard.

The Docker rebuild was initiated and monitored across multiple steps; the new container was confirmed clean with `mcp-servers:browser-access` and `monitoring:health-verifier` program blocks removed. A non-fatal Qdrant client/server version mismatch (1.15.1 vs 1.17.0) was also noted. Files edited: `combined-status-line.js`, `status-line-fast.cjs`, `status-line.md`.

theme: Rapid-LLM-Proxy Corporate Network Detection and Mode Labeling Fixes
summary:
A multi-step effort to correctly detect and label the corporate network in the rapid-llm-proxy and downstream health/display components:

- **Root cause (obs 43):** Corporate detection only checked RFC1918 ranges, missing the internal `160.50.x.x` range, causing the proxy to run in "public" mode.
- **Labeling fix (obs 45–50):** Renamed the conflated `'vpn'` NetworkMode to `'corporate'` throughout the codebase; updated type definitions, IP detection logic (`isPrivateIP` → `isCorporateIP`), and all comparison references across `src/types.ts`, `src/network-detect.ts`, `src/provider-registry.ts`, `proxy-bridge/server.mjs`, and `bin/start-llm-proxy.sh`. Backward-compatible checks accepting both `'vpn'` and `'corporate'` were added. A pre-existing TypeScript error in `provider-registry.ts`'s `initialize()` signature was also fixed.
- **Cascade fix (obs 51–55):** After renaming, `health-coordinator.js` and the system health dashboard only recognized `'vpn'`/`'public'`, causing `'corporate'` to fall through to `'unknown'`, triggering dashboard warnings and a broken LLM proxy status display. Added `'corporate'` to all valid-mode checks in `health-coordinator.js` and `system-health-dashboard`/`combined-status-line.js`.
- **Outcome:** Network mode now correctly reports `"corporate"` with `semantic_ok: true`; statusline and dashboard display healthy status on the corporate network.

theme: LLM Proxy Failures: Keychain, DNS, Corporate Proxy, and Network Mode Detection
summary:
A sustained multi-agent (opencode) debugging session traced LLM proxy failures through several layers of root cause:

- **Keychain investigation (obs 6–7):** Initially suspected a corporate password change had left the macOS login keychain out of sync, requiring `security set-keychain-password` and `gh auth login` re-authentication; keychain was then confirmed healthy and unlocked with no timeout.
- **DNS/proxy misconfiguration (obs 8–10):** `curl` failures revealed DNS not resolving due to missing `HTTP_PROXY`/`HTTPS_PROXY` env vars; proxy was started during a hotspot session without corporate proxy settings and needed a restart to pick up CN network config.
- **Supervision and hardcoded env vars (obs 11–14):** Attempts to restart `rapid-llm-proxy` (port 12435) were blocked by a launchd-supervised process (PID 42494) with `LLM_NETWORK_MODE=public` hardcoded, preventing corporate proxy routing; the controlling launch daemon was identified but not yet resolved in several exchanges.
- **Root cause and fix (obs 15):** Final root cause identified — the company uses `160.50.x.x` internal IPs which fall outside RFC1918 ranges, causing `probe_corp_network` to misclassify the network as public and strip proxy env vars. Fix involved editing the proxy startup/detection script to add the `160.50.x.x` range to the private IP detection regex.
filesTouched: (proxy startup/detection script to add company internal IP range)

theme: LLM CLI Proxy Network Detection Fix
summary:
Root cause identified: the launchd-managed `com.coding.llm-cli-proxy` agent always detected "public" network because DNS couldn't resolve the corporate PAC host from the home router context.

- Fix proposed and implemented: detect corporate network by checking if `px` is running on `127.0.0.1:3128` instead of relying on DNS resolution
- `start-llm-proxy.sh` edited to add localhost:3128 px-process check as fallback corporate network detection
- Requirements captured for three network states (home-no-VPN, home+VPN, office/CN) requiring different `HTTPS_PROXY` configurations
- `server.mjs` updated so LLM proxy's `detectNetworkMode()` <USER_ID_REDACTED> the health coordinator for network state first, falling back to local autonomous detection if unavailable

theme: Centralizing Network/Proxy Detection in Health Coordinator
summary:
Refactored network/proxy detection into the health coordinator as a centralized polled service, exposing state via `/health/state` so all consumers (statusline, dashboard) share a single source of truth instead of detecting independently. Text-only badges (`[N:HOME]`/`[N:CN]`, `[P:ON]`/`[P:OFF]`) were chosen to avoid emoji-width rendering bugs.

- Health coordinator updated to poll `location`, `proxy_running`, `proxy_functional`, `internet_reachable` on a 30s interval
- `buildCombinedStatus` in the statusline builder updated to render network/proxy badges
- Health monitoring dashboard updated to read from coordinator's network state
- Fixed a runtime crash: CommonJS `require()` calls inside an ESM module were replaced with proper ESM imports (`require('net')`, `require('http')`, etc.)
- Resolved variable collision where the Node.js `net` module conflicted with the local network state object — state references renamed to `netState` throughout lines 934–988
- Debugging revealed the coordinator inherits stale `HTTPS_PROXY` env vars causing probe failures when `px` restarts; PSM respawn cycle was used to apply fixes
filesTouched: coordinator source file to replace `require()` calls with ESM imports
health coordinator file (multiple ESM import fixes and `net.` → `netState.` variable rename corrections)

theme: Health Dashboard Offline Display Bug and Docker Startup
summary:
Health monitoring dashboard was not loading due to Docker daemon not running. Once addressed, the dashboard showed LevelDB, Qdrant, Stale PIDs, local proxy, internet, and `obs_api` as offline/erroring.

- Docker daemon was not running; options were to start Docker Desktop or run the dashboard standalone with Node.js
- `obs_api` was genuinely stopped and was restarted manually
- LevelDB, Qdrant, and Stale PIDs showing "Offline" were false negatives: the frontend `mapCheckStatus` function mapped `'unknown'` sub-check status to `'offline'` instead of a neutral "Unknown" state
- Frontend health status component (`mapCheckStatus` function and badge renderer) fixed to treat `'unknown'` as "Unknown" rather than "Offline", correcting false offline reports
filesTouched: frontend health status component (mapCheckStatus function and badge renderer)

theme: Health Dashboard Network Status Bug and obs_api Auto-Heal Gaps
summary:
Investigated three bugs: false "Internet Unreachable"/"Local proxy Offline" on the dashboard, `obs_api` not being auto-restarted when it stopped, and the root cause of `obs_api` stopping.

- **Network status bug**: `handleGetHealthStatus` in `server.js` passed `proxy` data to the frontend but never passed the `network` object, so `healthStatus.network` was always `undefined`; additionally, the running Docker Node process was serving a stale version of `server.js` without the fix — restarting the container resolved it
- **obs_api auto-heal**: `obs_api` was missing from `AUTO_HEAL_MAP` and had no remediation action in `health-remediation-actions.js` — both were added; separately identified that the `auto_heal` flag only enables a manual "Restart" button in the UI, not an automatic restart loop (only the LLM proxy has an FSM-based auto-heal in the coordinator)
- **obs_api crash cause**: stopped due to a Qdrant client/server version mismatch and a SIGTERM from an orphaned parent process, followed by a native fastembed crash during cleanup
- Files edited: `server.js`, `health-remediation-actions.js`

theme: Network and VPN Detection Logic Investigation and Fix
summary:
Investigated the existing network/proxy detection infrastructure and corrected a faulty VPN classification bug.

- Confirmed `coding/scripts/detect-network.sh` and `launch-agent-common.sh` already contain detection logic; the LLM proxy re-detects independently instead of consuming the shared result
- Root cause of incorrect "CN" label when on VPN: the VPN branch was an `else-if` that only triggered when PAC URL failed to resolve, but VPN traffic also resolves the PAC URL, so the branch was never reached
- Fix simplified the conditional: proxy running + `pacResolved` (corporate DNS) = VPN, since the proxy is only active when tunneling remotely
- Removed dead code (`hasVpn` variable) left over from the old logic
- Statusline and dashboard still don't surface the shared detection state — identified as a remaining gap

theme: Health Dashboard Network Field Redux Bug and Auto-Heal Gaps
summary:
Traced and fixed a chain of bugs causing the health dashboard to incorrectly report "Internet Unreachable" and "Local Proxy Offline" despite both being active.

- Root cause: Redux reducer dropped the `network` field from the API response, so the frontend always read `undefined`; compounded by an `as any` cast that hid the type error
- Fixed by adding `network` to the Redux state shape and removing the unsafe cast; frontend health dashboard component updated to use the typed field
- Secondary finding: `restartObsApi` was referenced in auto-heal action dispatch but never implemented, causing the Restart button to throw
- `restartObsApi` method added to the remediation dispatcher
- Auto-heal for obs_api was not wired into the FSM polling loop — only the LLM proxy had automatic restart logic; obs_api only showed a manual Restart button
- Stale Node process cache required a container restart for network data to flow correctly after the code fix
filesTouched: health status Redux slice to include network field
frontend health dashboard component to use typed network field
coordinator or dashboard server handler files
remediation dispatcher to add restartObsApi method

theme: Qdrant Client Version Update
summary:
Audited and updated the Qdrant client to match the running server version.

- Qdrant server was already at 1.17.0 with existing data intact; client was lagging at 1.15.1
- Updated client to 1.18.0, which supports the 1.17.x server; Qdrant handles data migration automatically on server startup so no manual migration was required
- `obs_api` restarted successfully with no version mismatch errors and data intact
- Noted that the `semantic-analysis` submodule also uses the Qdrant client and was checked for compatibility

theme: Health Dashboard Unknown Statuses, Stale PIDs, and Missing Sections
summary:
Deep investigation into four health system reporting gaps: LevelDB/Qdrant "unknown" status, stale PIDs, missing CGR commits-behind count, and online learning yellow state not reflected on the dashboard.

- LevelDB/Qdrant always showed "unknown" because the coordinator stored probe results in `levelDB.available`/`qdrant.available` but never mapped them to the legacy sub-check keys (`leveldb_lock_check`, `qdrant_availability`) that the frontend reads; restarting the coordinator resolved this
- Stale PIDs remained unresolved — the process check loop never probes actual process liveness; a patch to the coordinator's stale_pids handling was applied
- Unimplemented placeholder process rules (`process_uptime`, `high_cpu_usage`, etc.) were disabled to eliminate spurious "unknown" noise
- Knowledge pipeline yellow state has no corresponding dashboard section — queued for addition
- CGR commits-behind count identified as a separate gap; `0 commits behind` displays as a hash rather than a count

theme: Dashboard `toUiStatus` Mapping Bug Fix
summary:
Investigated why the dashboard continued to show stale/empty health data after earlier fixes.

- Root cause: `toUiStatus('passed')` fell through to `return 'unknown'` because `'passed'` was not in the function's recognized value mappings in `server.js`
- Fix applied to `server.js` to handle `'passed'` correctly; container restarted to pick up the change
- Prior to this fix the health-verifier check cycle appeared to not be running, but the real issue was all check results being silently mapped to `'unknown'`

theme: obs_api Recurring Crash Diagnosis, Auto-Heal, and Observation Backfill
summary:
Extended investigation into why obs_api repeatedly crashed, followed by hardening the service and recovering missed observations from the downtime window.

**Root cause of crashes:**
- `detached: true` + `unref()` orphaned the process to PID 1; macOS launchd sent SIGTERM, which triggered a C++ mutex crash inside the fastembed native module during Node.js cleanup
- `nohup`/`disown` approaches failed because the bash tool's shell exits immediately, orphaning the process before it stabilizes

**Fixes applied:**
- SIGTERM handler changed to use `SIGKILL` (or `process.exit(1)`) to bypass native destructor execution and avoid the fastembed mutex crash
- Switched to launchd with `KeepAlive: true` for persistent process management and automatic restart on crash
- Added obs_api auto-heal FSM to the health coordinator (mirroring the existing LLM proxy pattern), triggering restart on "unreachable" status
- Enabled `auto_heal: true` for obs_api in the rules config
- Created a launchd plist for obs_api

**Observation backfill:**
- obs_api downtime caused 21–120 sessions (May 1–14) to have zero observations recorded
- Most `[Raw]`-tagged sessions were correctly classified as low-value; genuine missed sessions required a different recovery path
- Backfill strategy: use `SpecstoryBatchConverter` with `TranscriptNormalizer.parseSpecstory()` feeding into `/api/observations/messages`, deduplicating by selecting the final ETM snapshot per session
- Timestamp-based matching needed since `sourceFile` metadata is not stored in the observations database; backfill script not yet executed
filesTouched: coordinator to add obs_api auto-heal FSM
rules config to enable auto_heal for obs_api

theme: Diagnosing and Fixing obs_api Downtime and Backfill
summary:
Both observations address the same work session: diagnosing recurring `obs_api` crashes and recovering missing observations from the outage window.

- **Root cause**: `fastembed` native code crashed on `SIGTERM` during C++ mutex cleanup; fixed by switching the `SIGTERM` handler to use `SIGKILL` to bypass crashing native destructors.
- **Process supervision**: `obs_api` moved under `launchd` with `KeepAlive=true` for automatic restart on failure; confirmed healthy on port `12436`.
- **Backfill**: 3 previously `Raw` observations were re-summarized; 8 remaining stubs confirmed as genuinely low-content (2-message sessions), not outage casualties.
- **Resilience**: A coordinator auto-heal FSM was added to handle the `obs_api` unreachable state going forward.

theme: Teams Channel Message Extraction, Auth, and Pagination
summary:
End-to-end effort to extract and report on messages from a <COMPANY_NAME_REDACTED> Teams Agentic AI channel using the Graph API via CLI tooling.
- Authentication initiated via **device-code OAuth flow**, requiring browser-based sign-in before data could be retrieved
- Initial fetch returned only 20 messages due to default `--limit` parameter; raised to 50, which is the **Graph API `ListChannelMessages` hard cap per request**
- Discovered message history was truncating at ~March 23 due to lack of pagination support in the CLI
- Investigated `nextLink`/`skipToken` pagination tokens returned by the MCP proxy; added full pagination support to `cmd_channel_messages` to follow tokens and retrieve all historical messages
- Final report saved as `agentic-ai-channel-report.md` and copied to `~/Downloads/`

theme: Teams Chat History Pagination Fix for Complete Retrieval
summary: Investigated why Teams channel message retrieval was capped at ~50 messages (back to March 23). Root cause was the CLI ignoring `nextLink`/`skipToken` pagination tokens returned by the API. Added a pagination loop to `cmd_channel_messages` that follows `nextLink` tokens until exhausted, enabling full message history retrieval.

theme: Link Index URL Resolution with Descriptive Titles
summary:
Goal was to enrich the report's link index so entries display descriptive titles rather than bare URLs or contributor names.
- Initially the assistant was fetching external sites; developer flagged that Teams message HTML already contained link preview titles
- Pivoted to extracting titles from Teams message metadata, yielding 299 title mappings and resolving 43 of 204 bare URLs immediately
- Remaining URLs resolved progressively using: URL slug parsing (93), YouTube oEmbed API (28), GitHub CLI, and arXiv API
- A background resolution process stalled on slow arXiv/GitHub API calls (121 resolved, 40 remaining at that point)
- Assistant debugged the stuck state and completed remaining work manually
- Final result: all 290 links in the index resolved with descriptive titles; Mermaid chart frontmatter also corrected from broken YAML syntax to `%%{init}%%` directives as a side effect

theme: Fix VKB Insight Format to Reference-Style Articles
summary:
Goal was to replace changelog/digest-style summaries in the VKB dashboard with structured reference-article insights (Purpose, Architecture, Use Cases, Troubleshooting sections).

**Initial misdiagnosis (obs 4):** Updated the insight generation prompt in `src/analysis/insight-generator.ts` and rebuilt the Docker image, regenerating 50 wave-analysis insights — but the VKB dashboard still showed old-format content.

**Root cause identified (obs 5, 6):** The VKB dashboard does not display wave-analysis LevelDB entities; it displays output from a separate **online consolidation pipeline** (Pipeline 1: ETM session logs → SQLite observations DB via obs_api → `ObservationConsolidator.js` → VKB). The previous fix only addressed Pipeline 2 (`insight-generator.ts`).

**Correct fix applied (obs 8–13):** Traced the full pipeline architecture and updated `_buildInsightPrompt()` in `src/live-logging/ObservationConsolidator.js` to produce structured reference-article summaries. All 47 existing changelog-style insights were deleted from `observations.db` (SQLite), and re-synthesis was triggered across 609 digests (14 chunks for the coding project alone).

**Status at end of day (obs 13, 14):** Re-synthesis was still in progress — 0 new insights written yet pending transaction completion. A further broad codebase investigation (obs 14) was begun to fully map the ingestion pipeline but ended mid-search without delivering findings.
filesTouched: all 47 existing insights from observations.db via SQLite
